studies.officialTitle: A Phase IIa, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of ASC30 Tablets and ASC30 Tablets A1 in Participants With Obesity or Overweight With Weight-Related Comorbidities

design.description: Seven-cohort parallel-group study evaluating two ASC30 tablet formulations and matching placebo.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: All gastrointestinal adverse events were mild or moderate, with no grade 3 or higher drug-related adverse events, no drug-related serious adverse events, and no hepatic safety signal reported.

sources.title: ASC30-202 study record (NCT07002905)
Ascletis' Oral Small Molecule GLP-1, ASC30, Demonstrated Placebo-Adjusted Weight Loss of 7.7% in Its 13-Week U.S. Phase II Study
Oral Small Molecule GLP-1, ASC30, Demonstrated Placebo-Adjusted Weight Loss of 7.7% with Better Gastrointestinal Tolerability in Its 13-Week U.S. Phase II Study

studies.officialTitle: A Phase I/IIa, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Single and Multiple Ascending Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of ASC30 Injection, for Subcutaneous Use in Participants With Obesity

design.description: Sequential single- and multiple-ascending-dose study of ASC30 subcutaneous formulations.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: In the latest 24-week phase 2 portion, there were no discontinuations due to adverse events; adverse events were mild to moderate, gastrointestinal adverse events were mild, and no hepatic safety signal was reported.

sources.title: ASC30-101 study record (NCT06679959)
Ascletis Announces Positive Topline Results from U.S. Phase II, 24-Week Study for Its Ultra-Long-Acting Subcutaneous Depot Formulations of ASC30 for Obesity
Ascletis Announces Positive Topline Results from U.S. Phase II, 24-Week Study for Its Ultra-Long-Acting Subcutaneous Depot Formulations of Small Molecule GLP-1R Agonist ASC30 for Obesity
ASC30, a Once-Monthly SQ Injected Small Molecule GLP-1RA in Participants with Obesity: A Phase Ib Study

studies.officialTitle: A Phase I Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Single and Multiple Ascending Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of ASC30 Tablets in Participants With Obesity

design.description: Sequential single- and multiple-ascending-dose study with five single-dose levels and three 28-day titration regimens.

studies.population: Adults aged 18 to 65 years with obesity and body mass index 30 to 40 kg/m2, without diabetes or other clinically significant disease.
studies.followUpDuration: 7 days after the 28-day multiple-dose treatment period
studies.safetySummary: No serious adverse events or grade 3 or higher adverse events were reported in the multiple-ascending-dose cohorts; gastrointestinal events were mild to moderate and no hepatic safety signal was detected.

sources.title: ASC30-102 study record (NCT06680440)
A Full Analysis of 28-Day MAD Study of Oral GLP-1R Biased Small Molecule Agonist ASC30 for Obesity
